content:
console.log is not the problem. The problem is using it as a replacement for thinking, tracing, debugging, logging, and understanding your own system.

10 min read
3 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Most developers do not debug.
They print.
They print random values, refresh the page, stare at the terminal, and hope the bug gets embarrassed enough to reveal itself.
That is not debugging.
That is panic with extra text.
I have seen teams waste hours on bugs that were not complex at all. A wrong environment variable. A missing cookie. A stale cache. A failed API response hidden behind a generic error. But instead of inspecting the real failure, everyone kept adding console.log in different places until the terminal looked like a crime scene.
The code was not hard to fix.
The debugging habit was.
console.log Feels Fast Because It Avoids Thinking
The reason developers use console.log everywhere is not because it is the best tool. It is because it is the easiest tool.
You do not need setup. You do not need to understand the debugger. You do not need…
